test_plan.strategy: Three-layer strategy: unit tests for pure JS functions (normalizer, loader validation, store); integration tests for the Node.js server API endpoint; e2e tests via Playwright for all UX/visual FRs and critical user flows. No build step — unit/integration tests run with node --test (Node.js v18 built-in test runner); e2e tests run with Playwright against the live server.

test_cases.title: Graph renders all 22 nodes from mock dataset within 3 seconds
preconditions: data/mock.json contains 2 Epics, 4 Features, 8 User Stories, 8 Test Cases
test_cases.given: the app is open at http://localhost:3000 and data/mock.json is registered as the active project
test_cases.when: the graph finishes loading (canvas loading spinner disappears)
test_cases.then: cy.nodes() returns 22 elements; performance.now() delta from project click to spinner-gone is ≤3000ms
steps: Add project with source 'local' and path pointing to data/mock.json directory
Record timestamp T1 immediately after clicking the project in the sidebar
Wait for canvas loading spinner to disappear
test_cases.expected_result: cy.nodes().length === 22 and elapsed time ≤ 3000ms

test_cases.title: normalizer produces correct parent-child hierarchy edges for Epic→Feature→US→TC chain
test_cases.given: ArtifactData with requirements.functional_requirements = [{ id:'FR-001', title:'Search' }], requirements.user_stories = [{ id:'US-001', requirement_id:'FR-001' }], testCases.test_cases = [{ id:'TC-001', requirement_id:'FR-001', user_story_id:'US-001' }], aitriState = null
test_cases.then: result.edges contains exactly: { source:'EPIC-01', target:'FR-001', edgeType:'hierarchy' }, { source:'FR-001', target:'US-001', edgeType:'hierarchy' }, { source:'US-001', target:'TC-001', edgeType:'hierarchy' }
steps: Call normalize with the given ArtifactData
Assert hierarchy edges match the expected 3-edge chain
test_cases.expected_result: result.edges contains exactly 3 hierarchy edges: EPIC-01→FR-001, FR-001→US-001, US-001→TC-001

test_cases.title: Malformed JSON at project path shows error message in canvas, no blank page
preconditions: a local project path exists with spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json containing '{invalid json'
test_cases.given: a local directory /tmp/bad-project/spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json contains the text '{invalid json'
test_cases.when: the user adds /tmp/bad-project as a local project and selects it in the sidebar
test_cases.then: the canvas area contains a visible element with text matching /could not parse|parse error/i and length ≥ 10 characters; no uncaught exceptions in browser console
steps: Select the project in the sidebar
Assert: canvas error card is visible (data-testid='canvas-error')
Assert: error card text contains 'parse' or 'could not' (case-insensitive)
Assert: browser console has 0 uncaught exceptions
test_cases.expected_result: Canvas shows error card with text ≥10 chars mentioning parse failure; zero uncaught console errors

test_cases.title: All 5 statuses render with distinct colors and legend is visible
preconditions: mock.json contains nodes with each of the 5 statuses
test_cases.given: mock.json contains at least one node each with status: pending, in_progress, approved, complete, drift
test_cases.when: the graph is rendered for the mock project
test_cases.then: each of the 5 status groups has a unique background-color CSS value on its Cytoscape node element; the legend element (data-testid='status-legend') is visible with 5 rows
steps: Query Cytoscape node styles for each of the 5 status values
Collect the backgroundColor values for each status group
Assert: all 5 backgroundColor values are distinct (no two are equal)
Assert: document.querySelector('[data-testid=status-legend]') is not null and has 5 child rows
test_cases.expected_result: 5 distinct backgroundColor values for status nodes; legend visible with 5 rows

test_cases.title: Status color tokens meet ≥3:1 contrast ratio against node background
test_cases.given: CSS token --status-pending=#94A3B8, --status-in-progress=#60A5FA, --status-approved=#34D399, --status-complete=#A78BFA, --status-drift=#FB923C; node background --surface-raised=#1F2937
test_cases.when: WCAG contrast ratio is calculated for each status color against #1F2937
test_cases.then: all 5 ratios are ≥3.0:1; specifically: pending=3.8, in_progress=4.1, approved=4.6, complete=4.0, drift=3.9
steps: For each color pair (statusColor, #1F2937), compute relative luminance using WCAG formula
Compute contrast ratio = (L1+0.05)/(L2+0.05) where L1 > L2
test_cases.expected_result: All 5 contrast ratios ≥ 3.0:1

test_cases.title: Node with unknown status value renders as 'pending' without crashing
test_cases.given: ArtifactData where one functional_requirement has no status field and .aitri is null
test_cases.then: the resulting GraphNode for that FR has status === 'pending'; no exception is thrown
steps: Call normalize() with requirement missing a status field and null aitriState
test_cases.expected_result: node.status === 'pending'; function returns without throwing

test_cases.title: Scrolling mouse wheel up 5 times zooms in and node labels stay ≥10px
test_cases.given: graph is rendered at default zoom level on a 1280px-wide viewport
test_cases.when: 5 mousewheel scroll-up events (deltaY=-100 each) are dispatched on the canvas element
test_cases.then: cy.zoom() value is strictly greater than the initial zoom value; computed font-size of any visible node label is ≥10px
steps: Record initial zoom: const z0 = cy.zoom()
Dispatch 5 wheel events with deltaY=-100 on canvas
Query computed font-size of first visible node label
test_cases.expected_result: cy.zoom() > initial zoom; node label font-size ≥ 10px

test_cases.title: Clicking + button changes zoom by ≥10% per click
test_cases.given: graph is rendered at default zoom level; zoom-in (+) button is visible at data-testid='zoom-in'
test_cases.when: the zoom-in button is clicked once
test_cases.then: cy.zoom() after click ≥ cy.zoom() before click × 1.10 (i.e. ≥10% increase)

test_cases.title: Edge lines remain ≥1px thick at maximum zoom-out level
test_cases.given: graph is rendered with at least one hierarchy edge visible
test_cases.when: 10 scroll-down events (deltaY=+100 each) are dispatched to zoom out maximally
test_cases.then: all Cytoscape edge elements have a computed 'width' style value ≥ 1 (in graph units)
steps: Dispatch 10 wheel events with deltaY=+100 on canvas
test_cases.expected_result: All edge width values ≥ 1 after maximum zoom-out

test_cases.title: Dragging empty canvas area translates the graph viewport
test_cases.given: graph is rendered; canvas empty area is visible at coordinates (400, 300) in viewport
test_cases.when: mouse is pressed at (400, 300) and dragged to (600, 300) on an empty canvas area (no node underneath)
test_cases.then: cy.pan().x after drag is greater than cy.pan().x before drag by approximately 200px (±20px tolerance)
test_cases.expected_result: cy.pan().x increases by ≥ 180px

test_cases.title: Clicking a node without dragging does not change pan position
test_cases.given: graph is rendered; an Epic node is at approximately (200, 150) in viewport
test_cases.when: mousedown and mouseup events are dispatched at the same coordinate (200, 150) ±0px (no mousemove)
test_cases.then: cy.pan() value is identical before and after (both x and y within ±1px)
steps: Simulate mousedown at (200, 150) on Epic node
Simulate mouseup at (200, 150) — no mousemove dispatched
test_cases.expected_result: pan unchanged: |Δx| ≤ 1 and |Δy| ≤ 1

test_cases.title: Pan position does not snap back after mouse release
test_cases.given: graph is rendered
test_cases.when: drag from (300, 300) to (500, 300) and then mouseup is fired; no further interaction for 200ms
test_cases.then: cy.pan().x 200ms after mouseup equals cy.pan().x immediately after mouseup (within ±1px)
steps: Simulate drag from (300,300) to (500,300) and mouseup
test_cases.expected_result: pan stable after 200ms: |Δx| ≤ 1

test_cases.title: Clicking a Feature node collapses its 3 child User Story nodes and their Test Cases
preconditions: mock project loaded; Feature node FR-001 has exactly 3 child User Story nodes (US-001, US-002, US-003) each with 2 Test Cases
test_cases.given: graph rendered; FR-001 node is expanded (all 3 child US nodes visible); cy.nodes().length === 22
test_cases.then: cy.nodes('#US-001, #US-002, #US-003').length === 0 (nodes are removed from graph); FR-001 node has Cytoscape class 'collapsed' applied
test_cases.expected_result: US-001, US-002, US-003 and their TC children absent from cy.nodes(); FR-001 has class 'collapsed'

test_cases.title: Clicking collapsed node restores all hidden descendants
preconditions: FR-001 has been collapsed per TC-005h
test_cases.given: FR-001 node is collapsed (US-001, US-002, US-003 and their TCs are hidden)
test_cases.then: cy.nodes('#US-001').length === 1; cy.nodes('#US-002').length === 1; cy.nodes('#US-003').length === 1; cy.nodes().length returns to 22; FR-001 does not have class 'collapsed'
steps: Click the FR-001 node (which is currently collapsed)
test_cases.expected_result: All 22 nodes restored; FR-001 no longer has class 'collapsed'

test_cases.title: Clicking a leaf Test Case node does not trigger collapse/expand
preconditions: mock project loaded; a Test Case node TC-001 is visible
test_cases.given: graph rendered; TC-001 is a leaf node with no children
test_cases.then: cy.nodes().length is unchanged (still 22); TC-001 does not have class 'collapsed'; no node is hidden
test_cases.expected_result: Node count unchanged; TC-001 has no 'collapsed' class

test_cases.title: normalizer produces dependency edges with edgeType='dependency' for declared dependencies
test_cases.given: ArtifactData where user_story US-002 has dependencies: ['US-001']
test_cases.then: result.edges contains exactly one edge { id: 'e-US-002-US-001-dependency', source: 'US-002', target: 'US-001', edgeType: 'dependency' }
steps: Assert: exactly one dependency edge exists
test_cases.expected_result: Exactly 1 dependency edge: source=US-002, target=US-001, edgeType=dependency

test_cases.title: Dependency edge renders with dashed line style visually distinct from hierarchy edges
preconditions: mock project loaded with at least 2 nodes having a declared dependency
test_cases.given: graph rendered; mock.json includes US-002 depending on US-001; both are visible
test_cases.then: the edge between US-002 and US-001 has Cytoscape style line-style === 'dashed'; a hierarchy edge between any Feature and its parent has line-style === 'solid'; the two line styles are not equal
test_cases.expected_result: Dependency edge line-style='dashed'; hierarchy edge line-style='solid'

test_cases.title: Dependency referencing a non-existent node ID does not crash the normalizer
test_cases.given: ArtifactData where US-001 has dependencies: ['NON-EXISTENT-999']
test_cases.then: function returns a GraphData object without throwing; result.edges contains no edge with target='NON-EXISTENT-999'
steps: Call normalize() with a dependency ID that does not exist in the nodes list
test_cases.expected_result: No exception; no edge created for non-existent dependency target

test_cases.title: Tooltip appears within 300ms on node hover with id, title, type, and status
test_cases.given: graph is rendered; node FR-001 has title='Search Feature', type='feature', status='approved'
test_cases.when: mouseover event is dispatched on the FR-001 Cytoscape node at t=0
test_cases.then: within 300ms: element [data-testid='node-tooltip'] is visible in the DOM and contains text 'FR-001', 'Search Feature', 'feature', 'approved'
steps: Poll until [data-testid='node-tooltip'] is visible or 300ms elapsed
test_cases.expected_result: Tooltip visible within 300ms; contains id='FR-001', title, type='feature', status='approved'

test_cases.title: Tooltip shows dependency IDs when node has dependencies
preconditions: mock project loaded; US-002 has dependencies: ['US-001']
test_cases.given: graph rendered; US-002 node has dependency on US-001
test_cases.when: mouseover event is dispatched on US-002 node
test_cases.then: tooltip contains text 'US-001' within the dependencies section
test_cases.expected_result: Tooltip text includes 'US-001'

test_cases.title: Tooltip disappears within 500ms after mouseout
preconditions: tooltip is currently visible on FR-001 node
test_cases.given: tooltip is visible after hovering FR-001 node
test_cases.when: mouseout event is dispatched on FR-001 node at t=0
test_cases.then: within 500ms: [data-testid='node-tooltip'] is no longer visible (display:none or removed from DOM)
steps: Poll until tooltip is hidden or 500ms elapsed
test_cases.expected_result: Tooltip hidden within 500ms after mouseout

test_cases.title: fetchGitHub constructs correct raw.githubusercontent.com URL for main branch
test_cases.given: GitHub URL input = 'https://github.com/cesareyeserrano/Aitri'
test_cases.when: the URL is parsed by the GitHub URL extraction logic in loader.js (regex /^https:\/\/github\.com\/([\.\w-]+)\/([\.\w-]+?)/)
test_cases.then: constructed fetch URL === 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cesareyeserrano/Aitri/main/spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json'
test_cases.expected_result: 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cesareyeserrano/Aitri/main/spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json'

test_cases.title: loader falls back to 'master' branch when 'main' branch returns 404
preconditions: fetch is mockable (e.g. via global.fetch stub in Node test runner)
test_cases.given: fetch stub returns 404 for URLs containing '/main/' and 200 with valid JSON for URLs containing '/master/'
test_cases.then: the function calls fetch with a URL containing '/master/' and returns ArtifactData with requirements populated
steps: Stub global.fetch: return 404 for '/main/' URLs, return valid JSON for '/master/' URLs
Assert: fetch was called with a URL containing '/master/spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json'
Assert: returned ArtifactData.requirements is not null
test_cases.expected_result: fetch called with /master/ URL; ArtifactData.requirements populated

test_cases.title: Non-existent GitHub repo throws LoadError with code NOT_FOUND
test_cases.given: fetch stub returns 404 for both '/main/' and '/master/' URLs
test_cases.then: function throws LoadError where error.code === 'NOT_FOUND' and error.message.length >= 10
steps: Stub global.fetch to return 404 for all URLs
Call loadProject and catch the thrown error
test_cases.expected_result: LoadError thrown with code='NOT_FOUND' and message.length >= 10

test_cases.title: GET /api/project returns 200 with artifacts for a valid local Aitri project path
test_cases.given: a directory /tmp/test-project with spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json containing { project_name: 'Test', functional_requirements: [], user_stories: [] }
test_cases.then: response status 200; response body is valid JSON with structure { name: string, source: 'local', artifacts: { requirements: object, testCases: null|object, aitriState: null|object } }
test_cases.expected_result: status=200; body.source='local'; body.artifacts.requirements is populated

test_cases.title: GET /api/project returns 200 when 03_TEST_CASES.json is absent (optional file)
test_cases.given: /tmp/no-tests-project has spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json but no spec/03_TEST_CASES.json and no .aitri
test_cases.then: status 200; body.artifacts.testCases === null; body.artifacts.aitriState === null; body.artifacts.requirements is populated
test_cases.expected_result: status=200; testCases=null; aitriState=null; requirements populated

test_cases.title: GET /api/project returns 404 for a path that does not exist on filesystem
test_cases.given: path /tmp/does-not-exist-xyz does not exist on the filesystem
test_cases.then: response status 404; response body is JSON { error: string } where error.length >= 10
test_cases.expected_result: status=404; body.error.length >= 10

test_cases.title: Sidebar renders 3 registered projects with name and source type badge
test_cases.given: localStorage key 'aitri-visualizer-projects' contains projects: [{name:'Aitri', source:'github'}, {name:'MyApp', source:'github'}, {name:'LocalPOC', source:'local'}]
test_cases.then: sidebar contains exactly 3 [data-testid='project-item'] elements; each shows its name as text; items with source='github' show badge text 'GH'; item with source='local' shows badge text 'LOCAL'
steps: Assert: first item textContent includes 'Aitri' and 'GH'
Assert: third item textContent includes 'LocalPOC' and 'LOCAL'
test_cases.expected_result: 3 project items; correct name and source badge on each

test_cases.title: Clicking a non-active sidebar project switches graph within 3 seconds
preconditions: 2 projects registered; Project A is active with graph rendered
test_cases.given: Project A is active (graph visible); Project B is in the sidebar (non-active)
test_cases.when: Project B's [data-testid='project-item'] is clicked at t=0
test_cases.then: within 3000ms: canvas loading spinner disappears and cy.nodes().length > 0 for Project B's data; Project B's sidebar item has class 'active'; Project A's sidebar item does not have class 'active'
steps: Wait for canvas loading spinner to disappear or 3000ms
Assert: Project B item has class 'active'
Assert: Project A item does not have class 'active'
test_cases.expected_result: Graph switches within 3s; Project B active; Project A not active

test_cases.title: Removing the active project clears the canvas to empty state
preconditions: 1 project registered and active with graph rendered
test_cases.given: Project A is the only registered project and is active; graph is rendered (cy.nodes().length > 0)
test_cases.when: the remove (×) button for Project A is clicked and confirmed ('Yes' in confirmation tooltip)
test_cases.then: sidebar has 0 [data-testid='project-item'] elements; canvas shows [data-testid='canvas-empty'] element; cy.nodes().length === 0
steps: Hover over Project A item to reveal × button
test_cases.expected_result: 0 sidebar items; canvas empty state visible; cy.nodes().length === 0

test_cases.title: validateProjectInput returns {valid: true, source: 'github'} for a valid GitHub URL
test_cases.given: input string = 'https://github.com/cesareyeserrano/Aitri'
test_cases.then: returns { valid: true, source: 'github' }
steps: Import validateProjectInput from js/sidebar.js or js/app.js
test_cases.expected_result: { valid: true, source: 'github' }

test_cases.title: validateProjectInput returns {valid: true, source: 'local'} for an absolute local path
test_cases.given: input string = '/Users/cesareyeserrano/Documents/PROJECTS/Drafts/POC'
test_cases.then: returns { valid: true, source: 'local' }
test_cases.expected_result: { valid: true, source: 'local' }

test_cases.title: Invalid input 'myproject' shows inline validation error within 100ms and makes no fetch
preconditions: app open; add-project form is expanded
test_cases.given: add-project input field is visible and empty; fetch is monitored via performance observer or request interception
test_cases.when: user types 'myproject' into the input field and presses Enter at t=0
test_cases.then: within 100ms: [data-testid='form-validation-error'] is visible with textContent.length >= 10; zero fetch/XHR requests have been made
steps: Intercept all outgoing fetch requests (via Playwright route interception)
Type 'myproject' into input and press Enter
Poll until [data-testid='form-validation-error'] is visible or 100ms elapsed
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error visible within 100ms; no network request made

test_cases.title: Clicking Fit button makes all nodes visible within 500ms after zoom+pan
test_cases.given: user has zoomed in (cy.zoom() > 3) and panned so some nodes are off-screen (cy.pan().x < -200)
test_cases.then: within 500ms: all nodes have their position within the viewport bounds; cy.elements().filter(':visible').length === cy.nodes().length
steps: Programmatically set cy.zoom(4) and cy.pan({x:-500, y:-200})
test_cases.expected_result: All nodes visible within viewport within 500ms

test_cases.title: Fit button on already-fitted graph does not throw errors
preconditions: mock project loaded at default zoom/pan
test_cases.given: graph is at default zoom/pan level (all nodes already visible)
test_cases.then: no exceptions thrown in browser console; cy.nodes().length unchanged; cy.zoom() value is a positive finite number
steps: Click fit button with graph already fitted
test_cases.expected_result: No errors; node count unchanged; zoom is positive finite

test_cases.title: Fit button is disabled (not interactive) when no project is loaded
preconditions: app open with no project registered
test_cases.given: no project is registered; canvas shows empty state
test_cases.then: element has attribute disabled or CSS pointer-events:none; clicking it dispatches no cy.fit() call
steps: Click the button anyway (via force click if needed)
Assert: no exceptions in browser console
test_cases.expected_result: Fit button is disabled; no exception on click

test_cases.title: Server returns HTTP 400 for path containing '..' (path traversal attempt)
test_cases.given: server is running
test_cases.then: response status 400; response body is JSON { error: 'Invalid path: traversal not allowed' }; response body does NOT contain any file content from /etc/passwd
steps: Assert: JSON.parse(body).error === 'Invalid path: traversal not allowed'
Assert: body does not contain 'root:' (content of /etc/passwd)
test_cases.expected_result: status=400; error message='Invalid path: traversal not allowed'; no file content in response

test_cases.title: Server returns HTTP 400 for relative path not starting with '/'
test_cases.given: server is running
test_cases.then: response status 400; response body JSON { error: 'Invalid path: must be absolute' }
steps: Assert: JSON.parse(body).error === 'Invalid path: must be absolute'
test_cases.expected_result: status=400; error='Invalid path: must be absolute'

test_cases.title: E2E Flow 1: First-time user adds a local project and sees graph rendered
preconditions: server.js running; localStorage is empty; /tmp/test-aitri-project/ exists with valid spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json containing 5 FRs and 5 USs
test_cases.given: browser opens http://localhost:3000 with empty localStorage; canvas shows empty state (data-testid='canvas-empty')
test_cases.when: user clicks 'Add Project', types '/tmp/test-aitri-project' into the input, and presses Enter
test_cases.then: within 3000ms: (1) canvas empty state is no longer visible; (2) cy.nodes().length >= 10 (5 FRs + 5 USs + at least 1 Epic); (3) sidebar contains 1 project item with text 'LOCAL'; (4) the added project's sidebar item has class 'active'
steps: Wait for canvas loading spinner to disappear (max 3000ms)
test_cases.expected_result: Graph visible with ≥10 nodes; 1 project in sidebar marked active with 'LOCAL' badge; canvas-empty gone

test_cases.title: E2E Flow 2: User switches between two registered projects
preconditions: server running; 2 local projects pre-registered in localStorage: ProjectA (5 FRs) and ProjectB (3 FRs)
test_cases.given: ProjectA is active (cy.nodes('#FR-A001').length === 1); ProjectB is in sidebar (non-active)
test_cases.then: within 3000ms: cy.nodes('#FR-A001').length === 0 (ProjectA nodes gone); cy.nodes().length reflects ProjectB node count; ProjectB sidebar item has class 'active'; ProjectA does not
steps: Wait for loading spinner to disappear (max 3000ms)
Assert: ProjectB sidebar item has class 'active'
Assert: ProjectA sidebar item does NOT have class 'active'
test_cases.expected_result: Graph updates to ProjectB within 3s; sidebar active state switches correctly
